title: The Bacterium in Your Mouth Has Been Found Inside Breast Tumors. Here's What It Does Once It Gets There.
description: A common oral pathogen responsible for gum disease has been detected inside human breast tumors — and new Johns Hopkins research reveals exactly how it accelerates cancer growth, corrupts DNA repair, and makes tumors three times larger. The effect is dramatically amplified in women carrying BRCA1 mutations. The science is compelling. The causality question is not yet settled. Both things matter.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Bacterium in Your Mouth Has Been Found Inside Breast Tumors. Here's What It Does Once It Gets There.
